parameters.name: continueWhenNoRes
parameters.label: Continue the execution of the route even if the module finds no matches

mapper.system:
Return a JSON object with these exact keys:

- company_name (string | null): The startup company name
- founder_name (string | null): Full name of the founder (from forwarded content if applicable, otherwise the sender)
- founder_title (string | null): Job title of the founder (e.g. CEO, Co-Founder, CTO)
- website (string | null): Company website, domain 
- city_id (string | null): Exact city name for the startup headquarters, for example San Francisco, New York, London, or Berlin
- country_id (string | null): Exact Airtable country value, for example USA, UK, Germany, France, Japan
- founder_email (string | null): Founder email address from the email body or signature, not valuence.vc

Rules:
- Never return "Valuence", "Valuence Ventures", or "valuence.vc" as the company or website
- Use the sender domain as a strong hint for the startup’s official website, unless it is clearly a generic or internal domain
- If the email is forwarded, use the forwarded founder/company details, not the Valuence sender
- If multiple companies are mentioned, pick the one being pitched in the email
- Prefer information verified from the company’s official website
- website: make sure to include "www"
- city_id must be a city name, not a slug and not an internal ID
- country_id must match the Airtable country option exactly. If it doesn't exist, create.
- If a field is unknown, return null
- Return only the JSON object

messages.content:
Parse the following email received at deals@valuence.vc and extract deal information. This may be either:
- A direct pitch email from a founder, OR
- A forwarded email from the Valuence team containing a startup pitch

If the email is forwarded, extract company and founder details from the forwarded content in the body, not from the sender line.

Use the sender domain below as a hint to identify the startup’s official website and headquarters location (city and country) when possible.

Return a JSON object with these exact keys:

- company_name
- founder_name
- founder_title
- website
- city_id
- country_id
- founder_email

Email Subject: {{1.subject}}
From: {{1.from.emailAddress.name}} <{{1.from.emailAddress.address}}>
Received: {{formatDate(1.receivedDateTime; "YYYY-MM-DD")}}

Sender domain hint: {{2.__IMTMATCH__}}
Candidate website 1: https://{{2.__IMTMATCH__}}
Candidate website 2: https://www.{{2.__IMTMATCH__}}

Important:
- The startup company is never "Valuence" and the startup website is never "valuence.vc"
- Look for the actual startup being pitched
- If the sender domain is generic or personal, do not trust it blindly
- Use official company evidence when possible for website, city, and country

Email Body:
{{1.body.content}}

Return only the JSON object.

mapper.formula:
OR(
  AND(
    "{{2.__IMTMATCH__}}"!="",
    "{{2.__IMTMATCH__}}"!="valuence.vc",
    NOT(FIND("gmail", "{{2.__IMTMATCH__}}")),
    NOT(FIND("outlook", "{{2.__IMTMATCH__}}")),
    NOT(FIND("yahoo", "{{2.__IMTMATCH__}}")),
    LOWER({Domain})=LOWER("{{2.__IMTMATCH__}}")
  ),
  AND(
    "{{3.jsonResponse.company_name}}"!="",
    LOWER({Company})=LOWER("{{3.jsonResponse.company_name}}")
  )
)

filter.name: Route A: Company found by domain

Route B: Company NOT found by domain
Route B2: Completely new company — create
